🧰 AI customization made easy
ALSO: How to animate 2D images
Read time: under 4 minutes
Welcome back, Superhuman
AI startup Magic has unveiled a new AI model that can devour roughly 750 books or 10 million lines of code in a single prompt. This could have deep implications for how we interact with AI apps and what we can use them for.
P.S. I’m down with a cold today so the newsletter is slightly shorter than usual and I’ve cut out the tools and image sections at the bottom. Those sections will be back next issue.
— Zain
Today’s Insights
Magic AI unveils a model with an ultra-long context window
Tutorial: Animate 2D images
Prompt of the day
From the Frontier: An AI-powered heart monitor
Everything else you should know today
NEXT IN AI
Magic AI unveils a model with an ultra-long context window
The name of Magic AI’s new foundation model is a bit misleading — to say the least. Despite its diminutive title, LTM-2-mini can handle prompts of up to 100 million tokens, or roughly 10 million lines of code, giving it one of the largest windows of any model.
What can it be used for?
Like other coding-focused models, it can automate complicated programming tasks and spot errors in long strings of code
But its larger context window means it won’t easily forget the code it was previously trained on
That accuracy unlocks new opportunities for software engineers
For instance, Magic demonstrated how the model can quickly build a custom calculator or password strength checker
What else is Magic working on? It’s partnering with Nvidia on a massive supercomputer that would dwarf the current record-holder — the US Department of Energy’s Aurora system. How will they do it? By stringing together tens of thousands of Nvidia’s state-of-the-art GB200 chips.
Riding the wave: Magic just got a boost from Atlassian, CapitalG, Jane Street, and several other VCs in one of the 30 biggest funding rounds for a US company so far this year, according to Crunchbase figures. The company joins a growing list of AI coding platforms that have attracted significant investment in recent months.

AI & TECH NEWS
Everything else you need to know today
Source: Getty Images
AI Bandwagon: OpenAI is reportedly in talks for a new funding round that could include participation from Apple and Nvidia. It’s a sign that the Sam Altman-led startup may have a big announcement around the corner.
Silicon Showdown: California legislators have passed the controversial AI Safety Bill, which would likely be a thorn in the side of Silicon Valley firms if approved by the state’s governor.
New Toys: The popular text-to-image platform Midjourney is building a team that will focus exclusively on developing new hardware projects.
Under the Hood: OpenAI and Anthropic agreed to partner with the US government to collaboratively test new models before they’re released to the public — a process that allegedly began this summer with OpenAI’s GPT-4o successor.
🧠 Brain Food: Australian researchers created a simplistic AI model with a synthetic jelly, then taught it how to play Pong. Rather than relying on an algorithm, the hydrogel used a basic form of “memory” to pick up on patterns and implement them into its gameplay. Its a groundbreaking step toward creating AI built from real-world materials.
THE AI ACADEMY
How to convert 2D images to 3D motion with AI
Go to Immersity AI and Sign up to get up to 100 credits.
Now Upload your 2D image and select 2D to 3D motion.
Adjust animation style, amount of motion, animation length, focus point, edge dilation and more from the editing panel on right side.
Once done, you can export your creation by clicking on Export button on top right side.
You can download it in MP4, GIF, or PNG format and share it with others.
For the example above, I have uploaded a 2D Rubik’s cube image and converted it into a 3D animation.
PROMPT OF THE DAY
Friday Funday: I Spy
Prompt: Let’s play a game of ‘I Spy.’ I’ll give you a clue and you have to guess the object I’m thinking of. The clue is: “It’s something you wear on your feet. [insert text here]
Source: WriteSonic
FROM THE FRONTIER
AI helps detect heart attacks years in advance
Source: Caristo Diagnostics
UK researchers say they’ve come up with a way to estimate someone’s heart attack risk up to 10 years in advance with the help of AI. Developed by a company called Caristo Diagnostics, the model can pick up on “biological processes that are invisible to the human eye,” including symptoms that CT scans can’t detect, Oxford Professor Keith Channon told the BBC.
The technology saves lives by giving patients far more time to treat underlying conditions and adjust their lifestyles. It could eventually be used to treat strokes and diabetes, too.
🧞Your wish is my command
Got more feedback or just want to get in touch? Reply to this email and we’ll get back to you.
Thanks for reading.
Until next time!
Zain & the Superhuman AI team
